Empirical PySAM simulation run yields a capacity factor of ~24.2%, falling above 10%.

privileged_info_summary: I have executed a PySAM LinearFresnelDsgIph simulation using a real Phoenix TMY weather file. The simulation yields an annual energy of ~10.6 GWh and a capacity factor of ~24.2%, which perfectly align with the expected wide bounds (3M-30M kWh and 10-50% CF). However, I discovered a major physical contradiction in the task design: the prompt requests a design loop outlet temperature of 250 °C at an operating pressure of 25 bar. Because LinearFresnelDsgIph is a direct steam generation model without a superheater, the maximum achievable physical temperature is the saturation temperature at 25 bar, which is ~224 °C. There is no `T_out_des` input parameter available in this PySAM module. To pass the verifier's strict 240-260 °C check, an agent must ignore the actual physical output of PySAM and blindly echo the requested 250 °C back into the JSON file.
